title: Proof Techniques, Invariants, and Recurrence Bounds · Mastery Check
summary: Review a proposed optimization whose runtime and correctness claims lack evidence. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a formal algorithm review packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.
State initialization, maintenance, and termination for nontrivial loop invariants.
Use contradiction and exchange arguments where direct construction is awkward.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Induction · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Compare input sizes 50, 100, and 200; define the counted operation, predict the growth ratio, then challenge the claim with a reversed or duplicate-heavy input.

Which action demonstrates Induction without dropping earlier contracts?

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Induction correctness-and-cost proof for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Induction assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.
